skill.name: assessment-builder
skill.description: Creates learning-objective aligned programming assessments with varied question types, Bloom taxonomy balance, diagnostic MCQ distractors, answer keys, and rubrics.
skill.summary: Builds balanced programming assessments with rubrics and objective alignment.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk matches, but review found they are false positives from markdown fences, educational terms such as evals, and rubric scoring text. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, or malicious intent was found; the only residual risk is a local validation script that reads a user-provided YAML file.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern Matches Dismissed as Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance matches occur in fenced examples, educational wording, or rubric text. Review found no executable shell calls, cryptographic code, network probing, or system reconnaissance behavior in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed these are documentation examples or assessment terminology. The executable script contains no shell, crypto, network, or reconnaissance logic.

low_findings.title: Local Assessment Validator Reads User-Supplied File
low_findings.description: The bundled validator accepts a command-line path, checks that it exists, and reads it with yaml.safe_load. This is expected validation behavior, but users should only run it on assessment files they intend to inspect.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read is direct and confirmed, but it uses yaml.safe_load and performs no writes, network calls, or command execution.

low_findings.title: Documented Command Runs Bundled Local Validator
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run the included Python validator against an assessment file. The command is local and purpose-specific, but it is still script execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented and points to the bundled validator. Review found no suspicious subprocess, shell, or network behavior in the script.

content.value_statement: Creating fair programming assessments takes time and often overuses recall questions. This skill designs varied questions, rubrics, answer keys, and validation checks aligned to learning goals.
actual_capabilities: Creates assessments aligned to learning objectives and success criteria.
Generates varied question types, including MCQ, debugging, code-writing, explanation, comparison, and project prompts.
Maps questions to Bloom taxonomy levels and targets at least 60 percent non-recall work.
Designs diagnostic MCQ distractors based on common student misconceptions.
Provides analytic rubrics, answer keys, explanations, and grading guidance.
Includes a local YAML validator for cognitive balance and assessment quality checks.
limitations: It focuses on programming assessments, especially Python examples.
It does not replace instructor judgment for grading policy or accessibility needs.
The validator checks structure and balance, not factual correctness of every question.
High-stakes exams still need expert review, piloting, and institutional approval.

use_cases.title: Create a Unit Quiz
use_cases.description: Generate a balanced quiz that covers objectives with MCQs, tracing, debugging, and short code-writing prompts.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing MCQs
use_cases.description: Rewrite weak answer options into plausible distractors tied to specific misconceptions.

use_cases.title: Validate Assessment Balance
use_cases.description: Review an exam for cognitive distribution, rubric coverage, question variety, and objective alignment.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Short Quiz
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5-question beginner Python quiz for these objectives: [objectives]. Include an answer key and explanations.

prompt_templates.title: Add Better Distractors
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve one or more multiple-choice questions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve the distractors for these MCQs. Explain the misconception each incorrect option tests: [questions].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Rubric-Based Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Create open-ended questions with grading support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a 60-minute intermediate assessment for [topic]. Include code-writing tasks, rubrics, answer keys, and Bloom level mapping.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Existing Exam
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a full assessment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this assessment for objective coverage, cognitive balance, question variety, distractor quality, and missing rubrics: [assessment].

output_examples.input: Create a beginner quiz on Python functions with six questions.
output: A six-question assessment with MCQ, tracing, completion, writing, debugging, and mini-project items.
Each question includes a Bloom level, point value, answer key, explanation, and objective mapping.
Open-ended questions include analytic rubrics for correctness, quality, efficiency, and style.

output_examples.input: Improve this MCQ about list mutability.
output: A revised question stem with one clear correct answer.
Three plausible distractors tied to reference copying, append behavior, and type confusion.
A short explanation showing what each distractor diagnoses.

output_examples.input: Check whether this assessment is balanced.
output: A validation summary with cognitive distribution percentages.
Specific warnings for missing rubrics, limited question variety, or weak objective alignment.
Concrete revision suggestions to increase application and analysis questions.

best_practices: Provide clear learning objectives, target audience, time limit, and desired question count.
Ask for answer keys, explanations, distractor analysis, and rubrics in the same request.
Review generated questions for factual accuracy, accessibility, and alignment with taught material.
anti_patterns: Do not request only recall MCQs when the goal is skill demonstration.
Do not use generated rubrics without checking local grading standards.
Do not run the validator on files outside the assessment materials you intend to inspect.

faq.question: What subjects does this skill support best?
faq.answer: It supports programming education best, with strong coverage for Python assessments and software development learning objectives.

faq.question: Can it create rubrics?
faq.answer: Yes. It creates analytic rubrics with criteria, points, performance levels, and grading guidance for open-ended work.

faq.question: Does it only create multiple-choice questions?
faq.answer: No. It supports MCQ, code-completion, tracing, debugging, code-writing, explanation, comparison, review, and project prompts.

faq.question: How does it improve distractors?
faq.answer: It ties each incorrect option to a likely misconception, so answers reveal specific understanding gaps.

faq.question: Can it validate an existing assessment?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks question variety, Bloom distribution, objective alignment, distractor analysis, and rubric coverage.

faq.question: Is the included validator required?
faq.answer: No. The validator is optional and local. Use it only on assessment files you choose to review.
